page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7T83 processor running at 2.55 GHz. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in several areas, particularly in CPU-intensive and memory-bound operations. The instance achieves top-tier results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks, as well as Passmark CPU encryption and extended instruction tests. Memory performance is mixed; cached memory bandwidth for small blocks is in the top 25%, whereas uncached large-block read and write bandwidths fall into the average range. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and small-model LLM inference speeds are also strong, while database operations, Redis SET operations, and medium-model LLM inference speeds remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r7a.16xlarge offers high memory density per vCPU, making it viable for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets without the added cost of local storage or GPU hardware. However, the lack of bundled local storage means users must provision external block storage, which may impact overall I/O performance and cost. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing power, though single-threaded tasks and uncached memory operations perform at average levels compared to the broader market.

This hardware profile supports memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale caching layers, in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and multi-threaded data processing pipelines. It is also capable of handling moderate LLM inference tasks for smaller models and general-purpose enterprise applications that require a high memory footprint.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor at 2.55 GHz. It is configured with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers strong performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks, cached memory bandwidth, and multi-threaded decompression, while uncached memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, and database operations perform at average levels. The baseline network bandwidth is 16 Gbps. This resource profile supports in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe storage. It also handles small-model LLM inference tasks efficiently.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core CPU and cached memory performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a.16xlarge features 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and in-memory databases.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform featuring sixty-four dedicated AMD EPYC cores and half a terabyte of RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 7T83 physical cores running at 2.55 GHz
512.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Strong multi-core CPU and cached memory bandwidth benchmark results
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Supports in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
